AI Clinical OS for India's Independent Doctors
Patient-owned records  ·  AI documentation  ·  Transplant coordination

aditya@ayuvinc.com  ·  +91-9599963641

2007
Aditya Kaushal · Founder & CEO
"At 17, my kidneys failed.
My father gave me his."
Read more  →
But my records didn't follow me. They came in hundreds of loose papers — scattered across clinics, carried in bags, stuffed into folders, recalled from memory at every new appointment. No system held my history. I did.
2022
Tunis, Tunisia · ICU
"Alone in a Tunisian ICU.
That kidney was failing.
I recited 15 years of history
from memory."
Read more  →
To doctors who spoke a different language. No records crossed the border. No continuity existed. The system had no idea who I was. I was a stranger in my own medical story.
2023
Dialysis · Open Heart Surgery · 11-month Wait
"After five surgeries,
I understood the real problem.
Every clinic owned my records.
None of them were mine."
Read more  →
It wasn't the doctors. It wasn't the hospitals. It was whose data it was. Patient data in India belongs to institutions, not patients. That is not a feature problem. That is an ownership problem. And ownership problems require architectural solutions.
RR
Raghu Raja Sharma · Co-Founder & CTO
"On the day his son was born,
Raghu's mother had a stroke."
Read more  →
For weeks he navigated a hospital system no one had mapped — which forms to file, which ward to find, which agent to trust. He had built enterprise systems at JP Morgan and HSBC. He had never felt more helpless. That week changed what he chose to build next.
KS
Dr. Komal Sharma · Co-Founder & CCO
"Every day in clinic,
her patients bring years of history
in plastic bags."
Read more  →
Photographs of old prescriptions. Half-remembered diagnoses. Referral letters from doctors she's never heard of. She cannot give the care they deserve without the data that should be there. She joined Dhara because she sees the gap from the other side of the desk.
Why We Build
"Three founders.
Three failures of the same system.
One architecture to fix it."
My mother gave me her kidney in 2024. I am alive because my family had the knowledge and resources to fight the system. Dhara exists so every patient in India has that — not because they're lucky, but because it's their right.
click to expand · navigate with buttons
The Problem
The Problem
Patient Data Is Imprisoned.
Not fragmented — imprisoned. Every clinic owns its own silo. Patients move. Care breaks.
"Data stays. Patients move. Care breaks."
See how Dhara fixes this  →
"Every doctor sees the full patient. Every patient owns their record. Forever."
Why Now
Why Now
Three forces have converged. The window to build India's health record layer is open — and closing.
₹

UPI Infrastructure

India digitized payments before healthcare. The playbook exists. Now it's healthcare's turn.

83%
digital payments via UPI
70%
UPI users outside Tier-1 cities
🏛️

ABDM / DISHA

Government mandating digital health records nationwide. Tailwind, not headwind.

79.91 Cr
ABHAs created (Aug 2025)
1.59 L
facilities on ABDM-enabled software
📱

Tier-2 SaaS Adoption

Independent clinics are finally adopting software. The distribution channel exists for the first time.

<10%
clinic software penetration today
Now
is the land-grab moment
"The infrastructure layer for India's clinics has never existed. The conditions to build it have never been better."
Sources: Praxis Global Alliance · ABDM Dashboard · RBI
धा
The Platform
The Platform
Dhara OS — Four Modules, One Architecture
Click each module to expand
Phase 1
Dhara / धारा
Patient-owned EMR · Longitudinal records · ABDM-native
Beta tested · 10-clinic pipeline
Every clinic owns its patients' data today — Dhara flips this. The patient owns the record. It follows them forever, across every doctor, every city. This is not a policy choice — it is the architecture. Competitors cannot retrofit this. It is the moat.
click to expand →
Phase 1
Vaani / वाणी
AI speech-to-text · Clinical summaries · Doctor-approved EMR
Sarvam AI · Live in beta · ₹65/hr cost · 62.9% margin
The AI scribe that makes EMR automatic. Vaani transcribes the consultation in real time, generates a clinical summary with contextual additions, and the clinician reviews and finalises before anything is saved. The wedge that gets every doctor through the door — and keeps them.
click to expand →
Phase 1
Setu / सेतु
Transplant coordination · THOTA-aligned · PGI Chandigarh pilot
In build · 15-week plan · 7 review gates
AI-assisted transplant coordination. Replaces paper forms and WhatsApp with structured workflows — waitlist, donor workup, NOTTO compliance, cold ischemia timer. Administrative support only; no automated allocation. Commercial after 6-month PGI stabilisation. SaaS per hospital.
click to expand →
Phase 2
Haath / हाथ
Hospital navigation agents · In-patient support · Caregiver connect
After Phase 1 EBITDA positive
When patients are hospitalised, families don't know how to navigate — tests, medicines, logistics. Haath connects them with vetted hospital social-service agents, directly and in real time. Unlocks after Phase 1 reaches profitability.
click to expand →
How It Works
How It Works
Not another EMR. A workflow that makes the EMR automatic.
STEP 01
Patient arrives at clinic
Checked in via phone or ABHA ID
STEP 02
Full history auto-loaded
Dhara surfaces complete patient timeline
STEP 03
Vaani transcribes the consultation
Speech-to-text captures clinical encounter live
STEP 04
Doctor approves AI-generated EMR
Nothing saves without clinical sign-off
STEP 05
Record persists permanently
Patient carries it to every future doctor, any city
"Patient-owned for life — even if they change doctor."

How We Calculated This
₹1.4L ARPC = ₹199/mo EMR + AI sessions + Rx Tx + Lab Tx blended
SAM = Tier 2–3 — Tier 1 served by incumbents; our wedge is underserved clinics
SOM = 300 clinicians — 1 new clinic/week in M1–6, accelerating via PGI network
Comparable: India healthcare IT ₹X Bn, 19% CAGR · Sources: Praxis, NMC Registry
Go-to-Market
Go-to-Market
Psychiatrists first. Then every independent doctor in India.
1
Months 1–6
Foundation
25 → 100
clinicians
Founder-led direct outreach. Every early clinic is a design partner. Build the product with them, not for them. PGI pilot begins.
2
Months 6–18
Wedge
100 → 1,000
clinicians
Vaani wedge + lab partnerships. Labs bring referring doctors. Psychiatrists expand to full EMR. Setu commercial at M12.
3
Months 18–36
Scale
1,000 → 5,000+
clinicians
Medical associations, PGI network, referral loops. Network effects compound. Each clinic brings 2–3 referrals. Pharmacy + pathology Tx engine live.
"Psychiatrists have the highest documentation burden. Vaani at ₹150–200/hr is immediate ROI. Every adopter naturally expands to full EMR — and brings 2–3 colleagues."

AI Intelligence
AI Intelligence
The Four Horizons
The data we capture today is the dataset that powers every horizon. No competitor can build this retrospectively.
Today
Live in Beta
Vaani — Speech-to-Text
Real-time transcription. ₹65/hr cost. 62.9% gross margin.
Horizon 1 · M6–12
Pre-Consultation Summary
Auto-surface full patient history before the consult.
Horizon 2 · M12–18
Pre-Visit Self-Assessment
AI questions before the visit. Doctor gets triage context.
Horizon 3 · Year 2–3
Precision Medicine
10K+ records → pattern recognition.
TODAY · LIVE IN BETA
Vaani — Speech-to-Text Documentation
Real-time AI transcription via Sarvam AI during the clinical consultation. Cost: ₹65/hr · Revenue: ₹175/session · Gross margin: 62.9%. The scribe that makes documentation effortless — and the wedge that gets every doctor through the door. Currently live in beta with 10 clinic pipeline.
🔐
Doctor Approval Gate — Always On, By Architecture. Every AI output is a suggestion. Nothing saves without clinical sign-off. This is not compliance — it is the product.
Traction & Roadmap
Traction & Roadmap
"25 clinicians proves the wedge. 300 validates the model."
Institutional Lead
PGI Chandigarh — Qualified
Pre-development discussion. Patient tracking + adherence scope. North SOTTO connection. Setu pilot anchor.
Pipeline
10 Clinics · Beta Pipeline
Diverse clinician mix across specialties. Real clinic testing. EMR + Vaani validated with real patients.
Product
Beta Tested · Setu In Build
EMR + Vaani tested. Setu: 15-week build plan, 7 Codex review gates, full TypeScript stack.

The Team
The Team
Aditya Kaushal, CFE
Founder & CEO
13+ years forensic investigations at Deloitte, PwC, Optum. CFE certified. Two-time kidney transplant recipient — father donated in 2007, mother in 2024. Patient Zero of Dhara.
Raghu Raja Sharma
Co-Founder & CTO
Enterprise architect across JP Morgan, Cigna, HSBC. TOGAF-certified. 14+ years, 7 countries. Built and scaled healthcare data infrastructure at enterprise scale globally.
Dr. Komal Sharma
Co-Founder & CCO
MD Dermatology, Medanta Fellowship. Consultant at Healing Hospital Chandigarh. Clinical conscience of the product — validates every medical decision before it ships.
Dr. Madhav Kumar
Strategic Advisor
Asst. Professor, Harvard Business School. PhD MIT. Expert in platform design and AI marketplace strategy. Advises on network effects, commercial architecture, and global scale.
"A compliance investigator who lived the broken system. An architect who builds at enterprise scale. A clinician who validates every decision. A Harvard platform strategist."
